header.title: Average Calculator — Mean, Median, Mode & Range Online
header.description: Free online average calculator to compute mean, median, mode, range, and geometric mean from any set of numbers. Enter data with spaces or new lines and get instant statistical results with visual breakdown.
header.keywords: average calculator, mean calculator, median calculator, mode calculator, find average, calculate mean, statistics calculator, average finder

input.title: Input Numbers
input.placeholder: Enter numbers separated by spaces or new lines

stats.stdDevPopSub: σ — based on all n values

howToUse.title: How to Use Average Calculator
howToUse.step1Desc: Type or paste numbers separated by spaces, commas, or new lines into the input field.
howToUse.step2Desc: Instantly see the mean, median, mode, range, minimum, maximum, and count of your data set.
howToUse.step3Desc: Scroll down to view standard deviation, variance, quartiles, RMS, geometric mean, and harmonic mean.
howToUse.step4Desc: Check the frequency distribution chart, skewness, and outliers detected using the Tukey method.

items.question: What is the difference between mean and median?
items.answer: The mean is the arithmetic average of all numbers. The median is the middle value when numbers are sorted. The median is less affected by outliers, making it a better measure of central tendency for skewed data.

items.question: Why is my standard deviation showing as zero?
items.answer: A standard deviation of zero means all numbers in your data set are identical. There is no variation among the values.

items.question: What does "no mode" mean?
items.answer: "No mode" means no number appears more than once in your data set. The mode is the most frequently occurring value, and if all values are unique, there is no mode.

items.question: Can I use decimal and negative numbers?
items.answer: Yes, the calculator supports decimal numbers, negative numbers, and zero. All statistics including mean, median, and standard deviation work correctly with these values.

items.question: What are RMS, geometric mean, and harmonic mean?
items.answer: RMS (Root Mean Square) is used in physics and signal processing. Geometric mean is useful for growth rates and requires all positive numbers. Harmonic mean is used for rates and averages of ratios.

items.question: How are outliers detected?
items.answer: Outliers are detected using the Tukey method. Any value below Q1 − 1.5×IQR or above Q3 + 1.5×IQR is flagged as a potential outlier, where IQR is the interquartile range.
